sourceMovie.title: Daddy's Home
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Daddy's Home for the same reason it broke through in 2015: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Sean Anders keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Home Alone 2: Lost in New York
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Daddy's Home clicked for you, Home Alone 2: Lost in New York (1992) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Daddy's Home?
faq.answer: Try Home Alone 2: Lost in New York, The Hangover Part III, Meet the Fockers—each shares DNA with Daddy's Home while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Daddy's Home?
faq.answer: Daddy's Home (2015) is best described as Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Daddy's Home part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Daddy's Home Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
